typecheck.output: Success: no issues found in 42 source files

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a carve-out to the 'Off-Limits' section for benchmarks/baselines/: dedicated baseline-refresh features (explicitly created to update baselines) are the expected exception to the 'never modify baseline files during feature work' rule.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature refresh-python-benchmark-baseline was specifically created to refresh baselines after Python 3.12→3.14 drift, but the AGENTS.md Off-Limits rule states baselines should only be updated via 'explicit benchmark-governance PRs'. The worker correctly followed feature instructions, but the rule text could be clearer about this exception.

rejectedObservations.observation: detect_environment_mismatch() only checks platform and machine, not python_version. The Python 3.12→3.14 upgrade caused baseline staleness.
rejectedObservations.reason: Already fixed by the benchmark-harness-stability feature which added python_version to the mismatch check.

rejectedObservations.observation: run_benchmarks.py records a single timing sample per scenario, causing noticeable run-to-run variance on Windows.
rejectedObservations.reason: Already fixed by the benchmark-harness-stability feature which improved to median of N=3 timings per scenario.

rejectedObservations.observation: The benchmark harness now has two separate subprocess invocation patterns (run_cmd_capture and run_cmd_timing) that independently set up PYTHONPATH.
rejectedObservations.reason: Ambiguous — this is a reasonable architecture choice (timing separated from parity capture) already noted in the code review. No actionable change needed.
